title: Wok-Seared Spring Korean Rice Gimbap
description: A one-pot Korean dish featuring buns, yuzu-cure, and a springy twist.
instructions: Heat a wok or iron teppan over medium-high heat.
Combine rice and water in the wok and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and cover the wok, simmer for 5 minutes.
Stir in Gochujang, soy sauce, yuzu juice, toasted sesame oil, and korean chili flakes.
Add zucchini, bell pepper, and small carrots to the wok and stir-fry for 3 minutes.
Add spring onions to the wok and stir-fry for another minute.
Cook the gluten-free soy sauce buns according to the package instructions.
To assemble the gimbap, spread a tablespoon of rice mixture onto each bun, then add a slice of zucchini, bell pepper, and small carrots.
Roll up each bun tightly and serve immediately.
Serve the gimbap hot, garnished with additional spring onions if desired.
